name: Ginger Bread Cookies in a Ginger Bread Bowl
recipeInstructions:
1. Cream butter and sugars.
2. Beat in molasses, dark corn syrup, and eggs until smooth.
3. In a separate bowl, combine remaining ingredients and blend.
4. Stir the flour mixture into the creamed mixture. The dough will be stiff.
5. Divide dough into fourths and wrap each in plastic wrap. Chill at least 2 hours.
6. Preheat oven to 350°F.
7. Cover the outside of an oven-proof glass bowl with aluminum foil.
8. On a lightly floured surface, roll out one of the fourths with a floured rolling pin into a 1/4-inch thick circle. Transfer dough to the outside of the bowl, pressing firmly onto it.
9. Use a small cookie cutter (any shape desired, such as hearts or holly) and cut out shapes around the bowl edge. Remember the bowl will be turned over when pressing in the design.
10. Place the inverted bowl on a baking sheet and bake at 350°F for 25-30 minutes, or until firm to the touch. Allow to cool on the glass bowl.
11. Carefully loosen foil from the bowl and remove foil and gingerbread bowl from the glass bowl.
12. Roll out remaining dough 1/8-inch thick and cut out shapes. Place on a lightly greased baking sheet and bake for 8-10 minutes, or until lightly browned. Cool on a rack.
13. Makes 1 gingerbread bowl and 3 dozen cookies.
14. (Optional) I weave a red ribbon through the shapes I cut in the bowl and tie into a bow. Fill with cookies and cover in colored cellophane.
